mechanism_narrative: A 3-month moving average increase in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) signals persistent labor market expansion, which raises household income expectations and drives aggregate demand, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day forward returns follow PAYEMS increases in fewer than 52% of instances over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is pinned at or below 0.10%, the zero lower bound eliminates near-term monetary tightening risk, reducing the equity risk premium and generating positive 60-day S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of triggered months over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month acceleration in industrial production (INDPRO) and retail sales (RSAFS) signals a broad-based expansion in both supply capacity and consumer demand, leading to positive earnings revisions and positive 5-day S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO and RSAFS both rise month-over-month
failure_condition: The joint condition produces positive 5-day returns in fewer than 54% of occurrences over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate is below 6.5%, further month-over-month declines signal an overheating labor market that increases the probability of near-term Federal Reserve tightening, causing negative 20-day S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Negative 20-day forward returns occur in fewer than 48% of instances when the trigger is active over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month drop in housing starts (HOUST) below 900 thousand units signals weakening residential investment and forward-looking demand contraction, leading to negative 60-day S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Negative 60-day forward returns follow the trigger in fewer than 50% of instances over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained industrial production (INDPRO) growth reflects rising manufacturing utilization and corporate revenue momentum, which drives positive 5-day S&P 500 forward returns as markets price in near-term earnings beats.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day forward returns following INDPRO growth do not significantly exceed zero (t-test p > 0.05) over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer prices (CPIAUCSL) combined with a flat or declining federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) generates negative real interest rates, which inflate equity valuations through lower discount rates and drive positive 20-day S&P 500 forward returns.
regime_hint: accommodative monetary policy with positive inflation
failure_condition: The interaction yields positive 20-day returns in fewer than 53% of months over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP (GDPC1) below 16000 billion chained 2017 dollars signals stall-speed economic activity that raises recession risk and produces negative 60-day S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Negative 60-day forward returns occur in fewer than 50% of quarters when GDPC1 is below 16000 over any 3-year rolling window.
